subtasks.description: Query SQL warehouse to retrieve Datadog license allocations, actual usage metrics (compute units, hosts monitored, custom metrics), and monthly growth trends across all service tiers

Reconcile allocated vs. actual usage, calculate utilization rates, identify over/under-provisioning by tier, trend growth patterns, and classify utilization posture (optimal, overprovisioned, underprovisioned) with recommendations

plan.cost_rationale: Two-subtask plan: (1) sql_gen (mid-tier) to fetch allocations and usage metrics with growth calculations—multi-table join likely needed across license, usage, and historical tables; (2) cross_recon (frontier-tier) to perform full reconciliation of allocated vs. actual, compute utilization ratios, identify provisioning gaps, classify posture, and synthesize growth pattern analysis with recommendations. Cross_recon is frontier because it requires multi-signal analysis (allocation, usage, growth) with inference and classification, not simply a join. No policy_action needed—analysis is informational. No additional extract/mongo needed—data is in SQL warehouse. Minimal decomposition: all reconciliation logic handled in one cross_recon call rather than separate subtasks.

output.sql: SELECT consumption_facts.license_id, consumption_facts.publisher, consumption_facts.product, consumption_facts.fiscal_year, consumption_facts.fiscal_quarter, DATE_TRUNC('month', consumption_facts.event_date)::DATE AS month, consumption_facts.event_type, SUM(consumption_facts.amount_usd) AS total_amount_usd, SUM(consumption_facts.daily_active_seats) AS total_active_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT consumption_facts.user_id) AS unique_users, COUNT(DISTINCT consumption_facts.event_date) AS active_days FROM consumption_facts WHERE consumption_facts.publisher = 'Datadog' AND consumption_facts.event_date >= '2024-01-01' GROUP BY consumption_facts.license_id, consumption_facts.publisher, consumption_facts.product, consumption_facts.fiscal_year, consumption_facts.fiscal_quarter, DATE_TRUNC('month', consumption_facts.event_date)::DATE, consumption_facts.event_type ORDER BY consumption_facts.license_id, month DESC, consumption_facts.event_type
